target_title: Code Review utilities to GitLab templates + baseline docs
Code Review utilities to GitLab templates + baseline docs

note.body:
Everything from here up generates content like the following:

```
# Code Diff Bundle

## Metadata
- Base ref: `origin/beta`
- Head ref: `e606be12`
- Merge base: `c9038a4b4ec540a2ce8d750c9bfb5152fa66de89`
- Project: `emss/maestro`
- Ref: `1447-disable-actions`
- Pipeline: `429042`
- Job: `code:diff`
- Job URL: `https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/maestro/-/jobs/3083988`
- Generated: Thu Feb 26 19:36:45 UTC 2026

---

## Diff Summary
 .gitlab/ci-includes/run-on-commits.yml         |   4 +
 app/history/ServerHistoryStore.spec.ts         | 243 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 app/history/ServerHistoryStore.ts              |  50 +++++
 app/history/ServerHistoryStoreThreaded.ts      |  12 ++
 app/history/ServerHistoryStoreWorker.ts        |   4 +
 app/history/systemActions.ts                   |   1 +
 app/server/appServer.ts                        |  46 +++++
 app/server/database/queries/documentHistory.ts |  43 +++++
 app/types/types.d.ts                           |   7 +
 app/web/Routes/Admin/AdminRoute.tsx            |   2 +
 app/web/Routes/Admin/EventEditor.tsx           |  52 ++++++
 11 files changed, 464 insertions(+)

### Totals
 11 files changed, 464 insertions(+)

---

## Commit List
e606be12 wip
60910d3d wip
76b45e4f feat: admin page to disable actions

---

## Changed Files (name-status, with renames/copies)
M	.gitlab/ci-includes/run-on-commits.yml
M	app/history/ServerHistoryStore.spec.ts
M	app/history/ServerHistoryStore.ts
M	app/history/ServerHistoryStoreThreaded.ts
M	app/history/ServerHistoryStoreWorker.ts
A	app/history/systemActions.ts
M	app/server/appServer.ts
M	app/server/database/queries/documentHistory.ts
M	app/types/types.d.ts
M	app/web/Routes/Admin/AdminRoute.tsx
M	app/web/Routes/Admin/EventEditor.tsx
```

Do we need all this? I guess printing the two points being diffed makes sense, just to help debug stuff. The diff simmary, totals, and changed files all seem redundant with the actual diff itself. The commit list could be confusing to the LLM if there's a commit message like "fixed bug with new button I added"...it might think that the MR is about fixing some bug, when really that was just a troubleshooting commit internal to the MR.

note.body:
The model has to be present on lambda.  Otherwise, you can change the model variable in the project's run-on-commits gitlab script.
variables:
  #OLLAMA_MODEL: "qwen3.5:35b"

target_title: Code Review utilities to GitLab templates + baseline docs

note.body: Can we change this on the fly? Or does it require a change on Lambda? I'm guessing picking a model that isn't currently on Lambda either (a) fails or (b) takes a while to download the model

target_title: Code Review utilities to GitLab templates + baseline docs

note.body:
Generally when I've fed diffs into Luna/Gemini I've just had to say `Summarize this diff and inform me of any issues: $diff`. Is all this other text necessary? Things that jump out to me as potentially not good include:

- Maximum 10 bullet points total.
- Do NOT explain obvious or small changes.
- What is "Test Gaps"?

target_title: Code Review utilities to GitLab templates + baseline docs

note.body:
<!-- ollama-code-review -->
## 🤖 Ollama Code Review (auto-updated)

The diff introduces several new files and modifications to the existing `.gitlab-ci.yml` file in the `emss/gitlab-templates` project. Here are the key changes:

1. **`.gitlab-ci.yml`**: A new job template, `code-diff.gitlab-ci.yml`, is included to generate a code diff bundle as an artifact. This includes metadata, commit list, changed files, and the full diff.

2. **`docs/agents.md`**: A new documentation file detailing the repository structure, safe edit zones, change hygiene rules, validation requirements, security rules, and guidelines for when to escalate changes to human review.

3. **`jobs/code-diff.gitlab-ci.yml`**: This new job template defines a series of jobs:
   - `code:diff`: Generates a code diff bundle.
   - `ollama:prepare_prompt`: Prepares the prompt for Ollama by sanitizing and formatting the diff.
   - `ollama:review_code-lambda`: Calls Ollama to review the code and generates a markdown report.
   - `gitlab:publish_code_review`: Publishes the Ollama code review as a note in the merge request.

4. **`sastignore.yaml`**: A new file specifying paths to be excluded from SAST (Static Application Security Testing) scanning, such as `coverage/` and `.git/`.

### Issues:
- **Security Concerns**: The `ollama:review_code-lambda` job includes sensitive information like API URLs and tokens. Ensure these are securely managed and not exposed in logs or outputs.
- **Dependency Management**: The jobs rely on external tools like Ollama and GitLab APIs. Verify that these services are available and properly configured.
- **Error Handling**: While there is basic error handling, consider adding more robust logging and notifications for critical failures to ensure timely issue resolution.
- **Documentation Clarity**: Ensure the `agents.md` documentation is clear and up-to-date with any changes in the pipeline structure or job definitions.

---
_Source branch: `code-diff-utils`_
_Commit: `a1177d3a`_
_Pipeline: https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/gitlab-templates/-/pipelines/429088_
_Job: https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/gitlab-templates/-/jobs/3084550_
